finding_results.notes: Petra now inspects concrete patch evidence on-screen, distinguishes the safer slow branch from the faster dangerous branch, names the job and license risk she takes by staying silent, and imposes a no-live-use-until-review condition.

The Stuttgart patient is now an anonymous young female gymnast with female pronouns, aligning this chapter to the packet-described Chapter 15 version instead of the earlier named male decathlete variant.
The full rewrite diversified sentence openings across the chapter. Verification after revision measured 32 'He' openers across 559 sentence units, about 5.7%, which is below the packet threshold.

The chapter now runs 4904 words while preserving the '# Chapter 9' heading contract. Added material comes from patch testing, virus forensics, and the expanded Petra consequence scene rather than filler.

summary: Chapter 9 was rebuilt as a longer preparation chapter with visible technical tradeoffs, stronger Meridian-signature evidence, and a more causally earned Petra alliance. Petra's cover now rests on observed patch data, an explicit professional risk calculation, and named conditions; the Stuttgart backstory now follows the packet-described female gymnast version; and the draft clears both the length and pronoun-rhythm guardrails.
